pick.rationale: Louisville holds a significant statistical advantage over Michigan State, ranking 16th–18th nationally in both Torvik and KenPom with elite offensive (20th–23rd) and defensive (23rd) efficiency metrics. While Michigan State lacks available advanced ratings, their Big Ten pedigree suggests solid quality, but not enough to overcome Louisville’s two-way dominance. Although Louisville previously faced uncertainty around star guard Mikel Brown Jr.’s back injury, the absence of recent reports indicating he’ll miss this game implies availability, preserving their full strength. Given the Cardinals’ superior metrics, ACC experience, and likely healthy roster, they are the more reliable pick despite Michigan State’s tournament toughness.

reasoningStep.title: Compare team quality and health status
reasoningStep.summary: Louisville is statistically elite; Michigan State lacks public metrics. No new injury updates suggest Louisville’s key player is available.
evidence: Louisville ranks 16th (Torvik) and 18th (KenPom) with top-25 offense and defense.
Prior pick noted Mikel Brown Jr. was questionable but no new info confirms absence.
Michigan State has no published ratings in either system.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
